label: Transformer–SSM & recurrent hybrid models

node.label: Transformer–SSM & recurrent hybrid models
node.definition: Hybrid sequence models interleave attention with state-space or recurrent blocks to combine content-addressable interaction with linear-time persistent state.
node.why_it_matters: They provide a design continuum between global attention quality and recurrent memory efficiency.
limitations: Block balance, state handling, parallelism, and fair scaling comparisons remain architecture-specific.

dependencies.rationale: Transformer–SSM & recurrent hybrid models extends or specializes the architecture family Transformers.
Transformer–SSM & recurrent hybrid models extends or specializes the architecture family Selective state-space models (Mamba family).
In this atlas graph, Transformer–SSM & recurrent hybrid models has a `composes` dependency on xLSTM & linear-recurrent hybrids with `common` strength.
Transformer–SSM & recurrent hybrid models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Gating & multiplicative control.

sentence_units.text: Hybrid sequence models interleave attention with state-space or recurrent blocks to combine content-addressable interaction with linear-time persistent state.
They provide a design continuum between global attention quality and recurrent memory efficiency.
Block balance, state handling, parallelism, and fair scaling comparisons remain architecture-specific.
